meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client builds. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional projects at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is 'boilerplate fatigue.' By leveraging a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you eliminate repetitive setup. Integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation allows you to bypass the blank-page phase, ensuring you ship functional, production-ready code in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor is the graveyard of ambition. You start with a vision, but by the time you've configured authentication, database schemas, and billing webhooks, the momentum has vanished. You’re not a bad developer; you’re just trapped in the 'plumbing phase' of software engineering.

True velocity comes from standing on the shoulders of your own infrastructure. By shifting from building from scratch to assembling from a hardened SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your focus for what actually matters: product-market fit and client delight. This is how the top 1% of freelancers maintain a high-velocity output.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Standardize your stack with a robust starter kit to ensure consistency across client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS starter kit with Next.js 15, PostgreSQL (via Prisma), and Stripe integration. Include role-based access control and a Shadcn UI component library.

steps.title: Automate Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models once and let the tooling handle the migrations and typing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for Organizations, Users, Subscriptions, and API Keys.

steps.title: Implement Auth Logic
steps.description: Secure your app immediately to avoid retrofitting security later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Clerk authentication middleware to protect API routes and dashboard pages, ensuring users are redirected to the billing page if inactive.

steps.title: Connect Payment Gateways
steps.description: Abstract the complexity of webhooks and subscription tiers into a reusable service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe service module that handles checkout sessions, webhook signature verification, and subscription status updates.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to rapidly build unique client requirements on top of your stable base.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a new feature module for a 'Project Analytics Dashboard' that fetches data from the PostgreSQL database and displays it via Recharts.

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a product: Version control it in a private repo and update it every time you solve a generic problem.
Always separate 'Infrastructure' from 'Business Logic' folders to make upgrading your starter kit easier later.
Use Claude Code's --dry-run flag before applying changes to complex files to catch potential conflicts in your base config.
Standardize your environment variables across projects to eliminate 'works on my machine' debugging.
Automate the deployment pipeline within your starter kit so every repo is 'deploy-ready' from the first commit.
content.next_steps: After deploying your first project, audit your code for repetitive patterns. If you find yourself writing the same function twice, move it into your starter kit library and update your Antigravity templates.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting incomplete projects drain your potential. Master the stack that scales your output and earns you more revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
